title: 7 Voiding and Refunding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
body_text:
7	Voiding and Refunding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
7.1	Overview of Voiding and Refunding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
Void a Money Order if an error is made while completing the blank Money Order or if the purchaser cancels the transaction during purchase.
If a Money Order is subsequently lost, destroyed, or stolen, the credit union can, at its discretion, replace or refund the Money Order.
7.2	Voiding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
Void a Money Order if the purchaser requests cancellation at the time of purchase or if the credit union makes an error completing the form (for example, exceeds the maximum face value). Proceed as follows to void a Money Order:
Write âVOIDâ across all four copies of the Money Order.
File the Money Order in the Completed Money Orders File.
Important
:	Donât discard spoiled or voided Money Orders.
7.3	Replacing or Refunding Canadian Dollar Money Orders
The credit union may be asked to replace or refund a lost, stolen, or destroyed Money Order. Doing so carries some risk since the credit union must honour the original Money Order, even if it is cashed after a replacement has been issued. It isnât possible to cancel or put a stop payment on a Money Order.
See Section 4.5, Policy on Replacing or Refunding Money Orders, for more information.
Considerations for Replacing or Refunding a Money Order
If a member reports that a Money Order purchased from the credit union has been lost, stolen, or destroyed and requests a replacement or a refund, the credit union may, at its discretion, do this for a member in good standing. There is no obligation to replace or refund Money Orders and this should only be done if the risk is low.
If the lost, stolen, or destroyed Money Order is later presented for payment or processed through the Clearing, it must be paid unless otherwise specified by the CPA (e.g. endorsement was forged). The credit union must then try to collect the funds from the member. It is strongly recommended that a credit union replace or refund a Money Order only when the purchaser
is a reputable and stable member,
has assets and/or income,
has a good credit history with the credit union, and
signs an Indemnity Agreement.
In other words, the credit union shouldnât replace or refund a Money Order unless it has been determined that the funds can be collected from the member if the original item is later presented for payment.
Important
:	Donât replace or refund a Money Order purchased by a non-member or if there are any suspicious circumstances.
If the payee reports the Money Order as lost, stolen, or destroyed, refer the payee to the person who purchased it. If the purchaser (member) wants to report the Money Order as lost, stolen, or destroyed, the credit union can replace or refund the Money Order as outlined below.
Procedures for Replacing or Refunding a Money Order
Proceed as follows if the credit union decides to replace or refund a Canadian dollar Money Order:
Confirm
that the Money Order hasnât been cashed.
Instruct the member (the purchaser) to complete and sign the Indemnity Agreement for Lost, Stolen, or Destroyed Documents, Form 1245. This form is available to credit unions that subscribe to the Operations Manual Program. The credit union can require that the member pledge security in support of the indemnity.
Credit the memberâs account and debit the credit unionâs internal Money Orders account. This reverses the original transaction.
If applicable, issue a new Money Order. Debit the memberâs account and credit the credit unionâs internal Money Orders account (i.e. the account number printed in the MICR line of the Money Order).
Set up a notation or other mechanism that will ensure that the lost, stolen, or destroyed Money Order is identified if it is later cashed and cleared.
If a Money Order that was reported lost, stolen, or destroyed is later presented for payment or processed through the Clearing, the credit union must pay the Money Order
even if it replaced or refunded the original item.
If the purchaser (member) still has an account and there are sufficient funds on deposit, debit the account (permitted under the Indemnity Agreement) or contact the member to collect the funds.
Related Information
Section 4.5
, Policy on Replacing or Refunding Money Orders
Indemnity Agreement for Lost, Stolen, or Destroyed Documents,
Form 1245
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
END
